skillName: Round integers to the nearest 10, 100 or 1000
misconceptionSummary: Students forget to zero digits to the right of the rounded column, leaving the original digits in place.

steps.narration: The student correctly rounded the hundreds digit from two to three but left the tens and ones unchanged. Any digits to the right of the rounded column must become zeros.

visuals.ruleText: Digits to the right of the rounded column become zero
visuals.subText: They are too small to matter at the precision you are rounding to

steps.narration: Rounding to the nearest hundred means the answer must be a whole number of hundreds. Tens and ones cannot survive — they are smaller than the unit of precision you chose.
Round the hundreds digit up from two to three, then replace the tens and ones with zeros. Three thousand three hundred — not three thousand three hundred and seventy-four.

visuals.label: Round up hundreds → zero the tens and ones

steps.narration: Three thousand two hundred and seventy-four rounded to the nearest hundred is three thousand three hundred.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: rounding the target digit but forgetting to zero everything to its right. The rounded answer must end in the right number of zeros — e.g. nearest 100 means last two digits are 00.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: After rounding the target digit, replace all digits to its right with zeros. A rounded number must be a clean multiple of what you rounded to.
